title: The Superseded
content:
I

    As newer comers crowd the fore,
     We drop behind.
    - We who have laboured long and sore
     Times out of mind,
    And keen are yet, must not regret
     To drop behind.

II

    Yet there are of us some who grieve
     To go behind;
    Staunch, strenuous souls who scarce believe
     Their fires declined,
    And know none cares, remembers, spares
     Who go behind.

III

    'Tis not that we have unforetold
     The drop behind;
    We feel the new must oust the old
     In every kind;
    But yet we think, must we, must WE,
     Too, drop behind?
meaning:
"The Superseded" is a quintessential example of Thomas Hardy's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
